name: Marinated Pork Tenderloin Birds
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine all marinade ingredients and marinate pork tenderloins overnight in the refrigerator. Remove from mixture, drain, and reserve the marinade.
2. Split (or pound) the pork tenderloins to about 1/3 inch thickness.
3. In a bowl, mix salt, pepper, sage, and chopped onions with bread crumbs.
4. Add butter to boiling water and gently incorporate the breadcrumb mixture until moistened but not soggy.
5. Mix in the egg into the stuffing mixture.
6. Spread the stuffing over the split or pounded pork pieces and tie with strings to form six rolled meat and stuffing birds.
7. Cook over medium coals for one to one-and-a-half hours, turning frequently. Baste often with the remaining marinade.
Credit: Steven R. Sutter, Bluffton, Ohio
